questions.explanation: APIs define how systems interact (endpoints, methods, schemas), enabling communication between services/clients and backends.

options.A: Provide a user interface for customers
options.B: Enable different software components to communicate via defined requests and responses
options.C: Store files and backups for long-term retention
options.D: Encrypt all data automatically without configuration

questions.question: In a typical web application, what is the primary role of an API?

questions.explanation: Authentication (Authn) confirms identity; authorization (Authz) controls permissions and access to resources.

options.A: Authentication decides what you can do; authorization verifies who you are
options.B: Authentication verifies who you are; authorization determines what you are allowed to do
options.C: They are the same concept; the terms are interchangeable
options.D: Authorization is only used for databases; authentication is only used for APIs

questions.question: Which statement best describes the difference between authentication and authorization?

questions.explanation: A primary key uniquely identifies records, enabling reliable joins, updates, and referential integrity.

options.B: To uniquely identify each row in a table
options.C: To automatically replicate data across regions

questions.question: In SQL database design, what is a primary key used for?

questions.explanation: CRMs track opportunities, stages, probabilities, close dates, and amounts—inputs used for forecasting.

options.B: Opportunity pipeline stages with expected close dates and deal values

questions.question: Which CRM capability most directly helps a technical sales representative forecast revenue?

questions.explanation: GraphQL can reduce over/under-fetching by letting clients request specific fields, often lowering the number of requests needed.

options.A: Use GraphQL so the client can request exactly the fields it needs in a single query
options.B: Use only SOAP because it is always faster than REST
options.C: Disable caching headers to ensure fresh data every time
options.D: Return HTML pages instead of JSON to reduce parsing

questions.question: A customer needs an API that mobile apps will call frequently over unreliable networks. Which design choice best improves efficiency and reduces round trips?

questions.explanation: E2E tests validate the full workflow across components; unit/integration tests provide faster, more targeted coverage.

options.A: Only unit tests, because they cover the whole system
options.B: Only integration tests, because UI tests are unnecessary
options.C: End-to-end (E2E) tests that simulate real user flows, supplemented by unit and integration tests
options.D: Manual testing only, because automation is unreliable

questions.question: A prospect asks how you would validate that a new payment workflow works end-to-end across UI, API, and database. Which testing approach is most appropriate?

questions.explanation: Managed Kubernetes services provide a managed control plane and support autoscaling for containerized workloads.

options.D: Content delivery network only (e.g., CloudFront/Azure CDN/Cloud CDN)

questions.question: A customer wants to deploy a containerized service with automatic scaling and managed control plane. Which cloud service category best matches this need across AWS/Azure/GCP?

questions.explanation: Circuit breakers, timeouts, and bulkheads prevent retry storms and isolate failures, reducing cascading outages under load.

options.A: Increase client-side retries with no limits so requests eventually succeed
options.B: Add a circuit breaker and enforce timeouts/bulkheads between services
options.C: Disable load balancing to keep sessions on the same instance
options.D: Move all services into a single large database to avoid network calls

questions.question: A prospect reports intermittent timeouts during peak traffic. Metrics show high p95 latency and frequent retries between microservices. Which change is most likely to reduce cascading failures in a distributed system?

questions.explanation: Layered automation (PR checks + post-merge integration/build) plus approval gates and progressive deployment meets compliance while keeping developer feedback fast.

options.A: Run all tests only after production deployment to maximize speed
options.B: Use a single manual step for every commit and skip automated checks
options.C: On each pull request run lint/unit tests and security scans; on merge run integration tests and build artifacts; require approval gates and deploy progressively (staging then production)
options.D: Deploy every commit directly to production with no rollback strategy

questions.question: You’re reviewing a proposed CI/CD pipeline for a regulated customer. They require fast feedback for developers and strong release controls. Which pipeline design best balances both?

questions.explanation: It clarifies scope with measurable metrics and likely bottlenecks, enabling a structured diagnosis and credible recommendations.

options.A: “Performance is complicated; you should rewrite the system in a faster language.”
options.B: “Can you share which endpoints are slow, the p50/p95 latency, request rate, error rate, and whether slowness is CPU, DB, or network-bound? Then we can propose targeted fixes like indexing, caching, or query optimization.”
options.C: “It’s probably your cloud provider; switch providers immediately.”
options.D: “If it works sometimes, it’s not a real issue.”

questions.question: During a discovery call, a customer says: “Our API is slow.” You have limited time and need actionable next steps. Which response best demonstrates strong technical communication and performance triage?
